name: Los Angeles
localTips: Get a TAP card (or tap a contactless card) for Metro rail and buses — the E Line now runs all the way from downtown to Santa Monica, skipping the 10 freeway entirely.
Read every word on LA parking signs, top to bottom — three stacked signs can each add a restriction, and tickets start around $73.
Morning coastal fog ('May Gray' and 'June Gloom') usually burns off by 1pm — plan museums for the morning and beaches for the afternoon in early summer.
Tipping is 18–20% at restaurants and a dollar or two per drink at bars; counter spots increasingly prompt for tips too, and 10% there is fine.
Distances lie: 'ten miles' can mean an hour in traffic. Cluster each day by neighborhood — a Westside day, a downtown day, a Hollywood day.

activities.title: Griffith Observatory at golden hour
activities.description: Free admission to the Art Deco observatory above Los Feliz — telescopes, the Foucault pendulum, and the classic Hollywood Sign and basin views from the terraces.

activities.title: Venice Boardwalk to the Venice Canals
activities.description: Muscle Beach, the graffiti-walled skate park and drum circles, then slip two blocks inland to the improbably quiet Venice Canals with their arched footbridges.

activities.title: Santa Monica Pier & beach path
activities.description: The Ferris wheel at the end of Route 66, carousel from 1922, and the beach bike path stretching south toward Venice — cotton-candy Americana over the Pacific.

activities.title: The Getty Center
activities.description: Richard Meier's travertine acropolis above the 405 — free admission to Van Gogh's Irises, Rembrandts, and Robert Irwin's spiraling Central Garden.

activities.title: Grand Central Market
activities.description: Downtown's 1917 food hall: egg sandwiches at Eggslut, birria at Villa Moreliana and carnitas stacked high — neon signs and a hundred years of LA appetite under one roof.

activities.title: The Last Bookstore
activities.description: A former bank turned two-storey book labyrinth on Spring Street — walk through the famous tunnel of books upstairs and browse the vault of horror paperbacks.

activities.title: Union Station & Olvera Street
activities.description: The last great American rail station — Mission Revival outside, leather armchairs and painted ceilings inside — then cross to Olvera Street's 1930s Mexican marketplace for taquitos.

activities.title: Hike to the Hollywood Sign views
activities.description: The Mt Hollywood Trail from Griffith Observatory climbs to the best straight-on view of the Hollywood Sign — with the whole basin, ocean to mountains, behind you.

activities.title: Hollywood Boulevard & the Walk of Fame
activities.description: Terrazzo stars from Grauman's Chinese Theatre forecourt (hand prints in concrete) to the El Capitan's neon — gloriously tacky, best after dark when the lights fight back.

activities.title: Urban Light at LACMA after dark
activities.description: Chris Burden's forest of 202 restored cast-iron street lamps on Wilshire — free, open all night, and genuinely magical once they switch on at dusk.

activities.title: Abbot Kinney Boulevard stroll
activities.description: A mile of Venice's best coffee (Blue Bottle, Intelligentsia), murals, and indie boutiques — GQ once called it the coolest block in America and it still argues its case.

activities.title: Runyon Canyon loop
activities.description: Hollywood's see-and-be-seen hike: a steep 2.7-mile loop with skyline-to-ocean views, off-leash dogs everywhere, and the occasional jogging celebrity.

activities.title: El Matador State Beach sunset
activities.description: Malibu's most photogenic cove — sea stacks, arches and caves at the bottom of a bluff staircase. Arrive an hour before sunset and stay for the show.

activities.title: Al pastor at Leo's Tacos Truck
activities.description: The trompo spins outside a La Brea gas station and the taquero flicks pineapple off the spit mid-air — LA's most famous $2 taco, served until the small hours.
activities.insiderNote: The original truck is at La Brea & Venice Blvd; go after 9pm when the pastor cone is properly caramelized. Cash moves the line fastest.

activities.title: In-N-Out planespotting at LAX
activities.description: The In-N-Out on Sepulveda sits directly under the LAX approach path — eat a Double-Double on the grass as 747s thunder a few hundred feet overhead.
activities.insiderNote: Order 'animal style' off the not-so-secret menu, and check flight-tracker apps for A380 arrivals — they come in from the east right over the parking lot.

activities.title: Tiki-Ti on Sunset
activities.description: A 12-stool tiki bar run by the same family since 1961 — 90 tropical drinks whose recipes are still secret, in a room the size of a garage.
activities.insiderNote: Order 'Ray's Mistake', the house classic invented by accident. Cash only, and if someone orders a 'Uga Booga' the whole bar chants — you'll see.

activities.title: Crate-digging at Amoeba Music
activities.description: The world's largest independent record store, now on Hollywood Boulevard — acres of vinyl, listening stations, and staff picks that outclass any algorithm.
activities.insiderNote: Check the in-store performance calendar — big acts play free shows on the tiny stage, and the trade-in counter is where locals sell, so bins refresh daily.

activities.title: 24-hour Korean spa day at Wi Spa
activities.description: Koreatown's famous four-storey jjimjilbang: clay and salt sauna rooms, hot and cold plunges, a full restaurant, and a rooftop deck — open around the clock.
activities.insiderNote: The uniform they hand you is your key to the co-ed floors; the ice room after the 231°F bulgama sauna is the ritual. Weekday mornings are blissfully empty.

activities.title: Bradbury Building lobby
activities.description: The 1893 atrium of wrought iron, open-cage elevators and glowing skylight that starred in Blade Runner — you can walk into the lobby for free on Broadway.
activities.insiderNote: Visitors are allowed up to the first-floor landing — enough for the full skylight shot. Come mid-morning when sun pours through the glass roof.

activities.title: Pastrami #19 at Langer's Deli
activities.description: Since 1947 opposite MacArthur Park — hand-cut hot pastrami on double-baked rye that regularly beats New York's best in blind tastings.
activities.insiderNote: Order the #19 (pastrami, coleslaw, Swiss, Russian dressing) and go before 11:30am to beat the lunch rush. Closed Sundays and closes at 4pm sharp.

activities.title: Late sets at The Comedy Store
activities.description: The black-painted Sunset Strip club where Pryor, Kinison and Rogan built careers — three rooms, and A-listers still drop in unannounced most weeks.
activities.insiderNote: The Original Room after 10pm is where surprise drop-ins happen; the names of comics who made it are painted on the wall outside. Book the cheap early ticket and stay.

activities.title: Mulholland Drive overlooks at night
activities.description: Pull-outs along the ridge road — Universal City Overlook for the Valley grid, Hollywood Bowl Overlook for the basin — a carpet of ten million lights.
activities.insiderNote: Hollywood Bowl Overlook is the classic; on summer concert nights you can faintly hear the Bowl below for free. Overlook gates close at sunset in some lots — street pull-outs stay open.

activities.title: Hidden bars of downtown's Broadway
activities.description: Downtown's historic core hides bars inside bars — a tiki speakeasy behind Clifton's Republic's giant redwood, and The Edison in a former power plant basement.
activities.insiderNote: Inside Clifton's, head past the taxidermy to the third floor and look for the unmarked door to Pacific Seas, a full tiki ship bar. Dress up slightly for The Edison's door policy.

activities.title: Universal Studios Hollywood
activities.description: The Studio Tour past the Psycho house and Jaws lake, plus Super Nintendo World and the Wizarding World — the only real working-studio theme park.

activities.title: Warner Bros. Studio Tour
activities.description: Golf-cart through the real Burbank backlot — Friends' fountain, the Central Perk set, active soundstages and the prop warehouse where movie history is shelved.
activities.bookingQuery: Warner Bros Studio Tour Hollywood ticket

activities.title: Academy Museum of Motion Pictures
activities.description: Dorothy's ruby slippers, the Dune sandworm, and an Oscar you can 'accept' in a simulator — Renzo Piano's glass sphere caps the movie museum LA always deserved.
activities.bookingQuery: Academy Museum of Motion Pictures ticket Los Angeles

activities.title: The Broad contemporary art museum
activities.description: Koons' Balloon Dog, Basquiats, and Kusama's Infinity Mirrored Room inside the honeycomb 'veil' on Grand Avenue — free, but timed tickets go fast.
activities.priceHint: free, timed entry; Kusama room extra reservation
activities.bookingQuery: The Broad museum timed entry ticket Los Angeles

activities.title: Petersen Automotive Museum
activities.description: Behind the ribbon-of-steel façade on Wilshire: Hollywood hero cars, hypercars, and lowrider culture across three floors — book the basement Vault tour for the rarest metal.
activities.bookingQuery: Petersen Automotive Museum ticket Los Angeles

activities.title: Natural History Museum & dino hall
activities.description: A growing T. rex series, the only real Dueling Dinosaurs cast, and the gem vault — in the 1913 building in Exposition Park next to the space shuttle's new home.
activities.bookingQuery: Natural History Museum Los Angeles ticket

activities.title: LA Phil at Walt Disney Concert Hall
activities.description: Gehry's stainless-steel sails hold one of the world's great concert rooms — hear the LA Phil under Dudamel-era acoustics that make every seat feel close.
activities.priceHint: $40–180; free self-guided building audio tour by day
activities.bookingQuery: LA Phil Walt Disney Concert Hall tickets

activities.title: Open-van celebrity homes & sights tour
activities.description: Two hours through Beverly Hills, Bel Air and the Sunset Strip in an open-top van — Rodeo Drive, movie-star gates and the guide's gossip doing half the work.
activities.bookingQuery: celebrity homes tour Hollywood Beverly Hills

activities.title: Whale watching from Long Beach
activities.description: Grey whales in winter, blue whales and dolphin megapods in summer — a two-to-three-hour cruise off the LA coast with naturalists aboard.
activities.bookingQuery: whale watching cruise Long Beach Los Angeles

activities.title: Disneyland Resort day trip
activities.description: The original park — Walt's Main Street, Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, and fireworks over the actual first castle. An easy (if trafficky) day trip to Anaheim.

activities.title: Dodgers game at Chavez Ravine
activities.description: Dodger Stadium at sunset — palm trees behind the outfield, the San Gabriels turning pink, and a Dodger Dog in hand at baseball's most beautiful ballpark.
activities.bookingQuery: Los Angeles Dodgers game tickets Dodger Stadium

activities.title: Summer night at the Hollywood Bowl
activities.description: Picnic baskets, wine, and anyone from the LA Phil to headline acts under the white bandshell — the definitive LA summer evening since 1922.
activities.bookingQuery: Hollywood Bowl concert tickets Los Angeles
